class.name: GodotMCPServer
class.brief_description: MCP (Model Context Protocol) server for Claude Code integration.
class.description: Implements the Model Context Protocol (MCP) to allow Claude Code to interact with the Godot editor. Provides tools for scene tree manipulation, script editing, and editor control. The server listens on a TCP port (default 6009) and communicates via newline-delimited JSON-RPC. A Python bridge script is used to connect Claude Code's stdio transport to this TCP server.

method.name: start
method.description: Starts the MCP TCP server on the given port and host. Returns [constant OK] on success.

method.name: stop
method.description: Stops the MCP server and disconnects all clients.

method.name: poll
method.description: Polls the TCP server: accepts new connections, reads incoming data, processes JSON-RPC messages, and sends queued responses. Called automatically by the editor plugin each frame.

method.name: is_running
method.description: Returns [code]true[/code] if the server is currently running.

method.name: get_port
method.description: Returns the port the server is listening on.

method.name: get_client_count
method.description: Returns the number of currently connected clients.

signal.name: tool_called
signal.description: Emitted when a tool is called via the MCP protocol.

signal.name: client_connected
signal.description: Emitted when a new client connects to the server.

signal.name: client_disconnected
signal.description: Emitted when a client disconnects from the server.
